title: aegilops triuncialis subsp. orientalis
intro: Aegilops triuncialis subsp. orientalis, also known as the wild goat grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The seeds of Aegilops triuncialis subsp. orientalis have been reported to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent cell damage, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is used to treat conditions such as digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, and is also believed to have potential health benefits in reducing stress and improving overall well-being.
